sprint: A Place With No Frame Chosen Now Renders With Zero Chrome, Matching Builder's Own Preview
task: Investigated a real, user-supplied screenshot pair: Builder's Working View showing a nicely-framed artwork placeholder for a Scene with two Places, vs. Creator showing the same Scene as two plain flat white boxes. Root-caused via debug instrumentation on a real compiled package (not guessed): the two Places had no Frame Variation assigned (holder.frame:null), and Creator's _resolveArtworkFields/_artworkBorder always fell back to the World's own top-level presentation:'gallery' preset's System Default look (white background, white-mat frame, soft shadow, gallery lighting) for ANY un-Framed Place -- correct per the Theme Language v2 architecture (Presentation Preset -> Theme Overrides -> System Defaults), but confirmed via direct product decision to be the wrong behaviour: the user wants a Place with no Frame chosen to render with zero chrome, matching Builder's own Working View (which never fabricates a themed look for an un-Framed Place). Confirmed via Museum Gallery's real, on-disk .vtheme that this change has zero visual effect on any existing shipped theme -- Museum Gallery's Showcase and Portrait Representations both set a real defaultFrame ('classic-white'), so every one of its real Places already has explicit chrome; only Quote has defaultFrame:null, and Quote's composition skips the whole Frame/border pipeline regardless. Fixed with one additive flag: _resolveArtworkFields now computes _hasExplicitChrome (true when a Frame Variation resolves OR the Story Author made a manual per-card Presentation override via Card Designer's own Presentation/Frame/Lighting/Composition controls; false otherwise) and _artworkBorder returns a genuine zero-chrome object (fill:'none', lineEnabled:false, shadowEnabled:false, design:null, _artwork:{}) whenever that flag is false, instead of resolving through ThemePresets.resolveHolder. _resolveWallTone is a deliberately separate resolution path (the gallery wall colour is a Slide-level concept independent of any one Place's own Frame choice, per Sprint 9.7) and stays completely unaffected -- a Scene's own wall tone/Scene-hosted background still paints correctly, now visibly showing THROUGH an un-Framed Place instead of being covered by a fabricated white mat. Verified via Playwright: a real Dual-Holder Scene with a navy Scene-hosted background and no Frame assigned to either Place now shows the navy correctly through both Place areas (previously solid opaque white); Museum Gallery's real classic-white Frame Variation still renders its white border chrome pixel-exact, unaffected. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite (29 scripts) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
